description: Provider-agnostic multi-agent project management: skills for Claude and Gemini + PTY overlay shell

tasks.title: Orchestrate skill — ledger-anchored rate cap derivation
tasks.description: Update the rate calibration logic in commands/orchestrate.md. Replace single-session delta method (cap = session_tokens / delta_pct) with ledger-anchored method: at session end, read agentflow_ledger.json, filter sessions where start_time >= window_start (local time; datetime.now() no tz), sum billable tokens (uncached_input + cache_creation + output per session), then cap_5hr = total_5hr_tokens / (end_pct_5hr / 100) and cap_wkly = total_wkly_tokens / (end_pct_wkly / 100). Window starts: 5hr = reset_time_5hr - 5h; weekly = reset_time_wkly - 7d. Gap: current session tokens not yet flushed to ledger — correct by adding (end_pct - start_pct) * prior_cap_estimate as current-session delta if ledger read returns fewer tokens than expected. Write updated cap estimates to ~/.agentflow/rate_calibration.json alongside existing EWMA fields.

unit: commands/orchestrate.md instructs ledger read at session end
commands/orchestrate.md filters sessions by start_time >= window_start (local time)
commands/orchestrate.md sums uncached_input + cache_creation + output per session
commands/orchestrate.md derives cap_5hr = total_5hr_tokens / (end_pct_5hr / 100)
commands/orchestrate.md derives cap_wkly = total_wkly_tokens / (end_pct_wkly / 100)
commands/orchestrate.md documents current-session gap and correction

tasks.acceptance_criteria: orchestrate.md rate calibration write uses ledger-anchored cap derivation; window start computation documented; current-session gap correction present

tasks.title: Oracle skill — general .idx reading protocol
tasks.description: Add a general Targeted Reads Rule section to commands/oracle.md. Currently oracle only checks .idx for architecture.md (Step 2a). All other file reads (lazy-loaded phase files: market.md 63L, checklist.md 113L, generation.md 133L; any future reads) have no .idx protocol. Add a 'Targeted Reads Rule' section matching the structure in commands/worker/system.md lines 51-78: compute HASH, check IDX path, grep for symbol, Read with offset+limit, fallback to full read if absent. Note: oracle's phase files (market.md, checklist.md, generation.md) are intentionally read in full when entering each phase — the rule applies to any targeted symbol/section lookup oracle makes outside of those full-phase loads.

unit: commands/oracle.md contains a Targeted Reads Rule section
commands/oracle.md Targeted Reads Rule instructs HASH computation
commands/oracle.md Targeted Reads Rule instructs grep for symbol in .idx
commands/oracle.md Targeted Reads Rule instructs Read with offset+limit
commands/oracle.md Targeted Reads Rule has fallback to full read when .idx absent

tasks.acceptance_criteria: oracle.md contains a general Targeted Reads Rule matching the worker protocol; existing Step 2a architecture.md-specific section preserved

tasks.title: Add agentflow/__main__.py so `python -m agentflow` is executable
tasks.description: Create agentflow/__main__.py as a thin shim that imports and calls agentflow.cli.main(). Currently `python -m agentflow shell` (and all subcommands) fail with 'No module named agentflow.__main__; agentflow is a package and cannot be directly executed'. The fix is a 2-line __main__.py: `from agentflow.cli import main; main()`. No logic — delegate entirely to cli.main().

unit: python -m agentflow --help exits 0 and prints usage
python -m agentflow shell --help exits 0 and includes 'PTY overlay shell'
__main__.py contains no logic beyond import + main() call

tasks.acceptance_criteria: `python -m agentflow --help` and `python -m agentflow shell` work without error

tasks.title: PTY session manager — Read-event idx injection + proactive verbosity banners
tasks.description: Two additions to SessionManager, no new files. (1) Read-event idx injection: in _handle_output, strip ANSI codes (re.sub), regex-match for Read + file path pattern, check if ~/.agentflow/cache/<hash>/index/<file>.idx exists, inject targeted [IDX] <file>.idx banner immediately if found. Add _ansi_strip(text) and _detect_read_path(text) as private helpers. Existing every-3-turns injection kept as fallback. (2) Proactive verbosity injection: inject verbosity constraint banner once at session start (write to PTY stdin before first user input); re-inject every ~60s via time.monotonic() timer checked in the relay loop — no turn counting, no output parsing. Banner text: "[VERBOSITY] Target <=3 sentences (~150 tokens) per response." Timer state held in SessionManager; relay loop calls session_manager.tick() each iteration.

unit: _ansi_strip strips standard ANSI escape sequences, leaves plain text unchanged
_detect_read_path returns file path when Read <path> pattern present in clean text
_detect_read_path returns None when no Read pattern found
_handle_output injects targeted [IDX] banner when .idx exists for detected file
_handle_output does not inject banner when no .idx exists for detected file
tick() injects verbosity banner when elapsed >= 60s
tick() does not inject when elapsed < 60s
existing every-3-turns idx injection still fires at turn boundaries

tasks.acceptance_criteria: pytest tests/ passes; Read tool call for indexed file triggers targeted [IDX] banner; verbosity banner injected at session start and every ~60s; session_manager.py stays under 250 lines

tasks.title: Bug fixes: verbosity banner timing + absolute-path IDX guard
tasks.description: Two bugs found and fixed in session_manager.py. (1) Static verbosity banner fired at __init__ time before child process was live — landed in input field on restart. Fixed by deferring injection to first _handle_output chunk. Added _initial_banner_sent flag. (2) T-052 IDX reactive banner never fired for real Read calls because _handle_output filtered out paths starting with '/' — but Read always uses absolute paths. Fixed by stripping cwd prefix from absolute paths before idx lookup; paths outside cwd silently dropped. Three pre-existing test failures fixed (verbosity threshold test missing 30s cooldown bypass; two tick() tests referencing removed method replaced with banner-timing tests).

tasks.title: read_check.py: skip block on empty idx + compress output message
tasks.description: Two fixes to read_check.py PreToolUse hook. (1) False-positive block: when .idx exists but is 0 bytes (file <50 lines or no indexable sections), hook still fires sys.exit(1) — add short-circuit after strip() so empty idx allows full read through. (2) Verbose output: current 3-line error message (~50 tokens) fires into model context on every blocked read; compress to single telegraphic line.

tasks.title: Reorganise skill dirs: commands/claude/ + commands/gemini/
tasks.description: Restructure skill file locations to track Claude and Gemini skills under a unified commands/ tree. (1) Move all files under commands/ to commands/claude/ — preserving subdirectory structure (oracle/, orchestrator/, reviewer/, worker/). (2) Move Gemini skills from .agents/ to commands/gemini/ — AGENTS.md → commands/gemini/AGENTS.md; .agents/skills/drift/, handoff/, orchestrate/ → commands/gemini/skills/drift/, handoff/, orchestrate/. (3) Update all path references: design_status.md (Skill file location row), architecture.md (commands/ references in structure section and provider table), CLAUDE.md (structure block, skills dir entry), tasks.json (owns + context_section fields referencing commands/*.md), tests/prompts/test_oracle_prompts.py + test_reviewer_prompts.py + test_worker_prompts.py (ORACLE_DIR, REVIEWER_DIR, ORCHESTRATOR_DIR, WORKER_DIR constants). (4) Regenerate .idx cache: after moves, run the inline indexer against commands/claude/ and commands/gemini/ so ~/.agentflow/cache/<hash>/index/commands/claude/*.idx entries replace the stale commands/*.idx entries. Stale commands/*.idx entries must be deleted. (5) Copy target: update any copy instructions from commands/ → ~/.claude/commands/ to read from commands/claude/ instead.

tasks.title: Align Gemini orchestrate and handoff skills with Claude equivalents
tasks.description: Establish parity between Gemini skills under commands/gemini/skills/ and their Claude equivalents under commands/claude/. (1) Port the advanced ledger-anchored rate cap derivation logic (T-039/T-042) to commands/gemini/skills/orchestrate/SKILL.md, replacing the single-session delta heuristic. (2) Align targeted reads index rules, heuristics, and prompt boundaries in the Gemini orchestrate skill, compressing/refactoring where needed to respect prompt line limits. (3) Sync step details, worktree cleanup checks, and output formats in commands/gemini/skills/handoff/SKILL.md.

tasks.title: PostToolUse hook: block Write/Edit when file exceeds line limit
tasks.description: New hook agentflow/hooks/size_check.py — PostToolUse on Write and Edit. After each write, read the modified file and check line count against per-category limits: tests/ → 350, commands/ → 150, all other .py → 250. Stubs excluded: files whose content is >50% pass/ellipsis lines skip the check. On violation: print single telegraphic message and exit(1) to block next tool call — "FILE TOO LARGE: <rel_path> is <N> lines (limit <L>) — split by responsibility boundary before proceeding." On pass: exit(0), no output. Register under PostToolUse for both Write and Edit matchers in .claude/settings.json alongside write_indexer. Add tests/hooks/test_size_check.py covering: under-limit passes, over-limit blocks, correct limit per category, stub exemption, non-.py files skipped.

tasks.title: Bake verbosity target into skill prompts as standing instruction
tasks.description: Add a consistent verbosity target to oracle.md and orchestrate.md as a permanent standing instruction, not an optional argument. oracle.md: promote the Phase 2 verbosity note ("≤3 sentences per exchange") to a top-level instruction visible before any phase executes — add "≤3 sentences (~150 tokens) per response; expand only if user asks" near the opening. Remove the duplicate in Phase 2. orchestrate.md: extend the existing "## Verbosity rules" section to include the token target — "≤3 sentences (~150 tokens) per orchestrator status message". Both changes must survive context growth (instruction must appear early in the file, not only in a late section). No changes to handoff.md, drift.md, or worker prompts — those are single-purpose and do not have multi-turn verbosity risk.
